failed_cases.description: Requirement parses direct URLs and marker text as distinct fields.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('Demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"')
print(r.name)
print(r.url)
print(str(r.specifier))
print(type(r.marker).__name__)
print(str(r))
failed_cases.expected_output:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl; python_version >= "3.10"
failed_cases.actual:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl ; python_version >= "3.10"
failed_cases.expected:
demo
https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl

Marker
demo @ https://example.test/demo-1.0.whl; python_version >= "3.10"

failed_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

all_cases.description: Public names import from the single module and exception classes inherit from ValueError.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version, InvalidVersion, SpecifierSet, InvalidSpecifier, Requirement, InvalidRequirement, Marker, InvalidMarker, UndefinedEnvironmentName, default_environment, is_requirement_satisfied
print(Version.__name__, SpecifierSet.__name__, Requirement.__name__, Marker.__name__)
print(issubclass(InvalidVersion, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidSpecifier, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidRequirement, ValueError), issubclass(InvalidMarker, ValueError), issubclass(UndefinedEnvironmentName, ValueError))
all_cases.expected_output:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True
all_cases.actual:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True
all_cases.expected:
Version SpecifierSet Requirement Marker
True True True True True

all_cases.description: Version canonicalizes release zeros, epoch, pre-release aliases, post-release aliases, and local labels.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
vals = ['1.0.0', '2!01.02.0', '1.0ALPHA2', '1.0-r3', '1.0+LOCAL_build-5']
print([str(Version(v)) for v in vals])
print(Version('1.0') == Version('1.0.0'))

all_cases.description: Version ordering handles dev, pre, final, and post releases.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
ordered = [Version(v) for v in ['1.0.dev1', '1.0a1', '1.0b1', '1.0rc1', '1.0', '1.0.post1']]
print(all(a < b for a, b in zip(ordered, ordered[1:])))
print([str(v) for v in sorted(reversed(ordered))])

all_cases.description: Version ordering includes epochs and local labels after public-version equality.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Version
print(Version('1!0.9') > Version('2.0'))
print(Version('1.0+abc') < Version('1.0+1'))
print(Version('1.0+1') < Version('1.0+2'))

all_cases.description: Invalid version text raises InvalidVersion.
SpecifierSet applies inclusive and exclusive range clauses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.2, <2.0')
print(str(s))
print([s.contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2', '1.9', '2.0']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports equality, inequality, and wildcard release prefixes.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
print([SpecifierSet('==1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.2', '1.2.5', '1.3']])
print([SpecifierSet('!=1.2.*').contains(v) for v in ['1.1', '1.2.9', '1.3']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet supports compatible release bounds.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s1 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4')
s2 = SpecifierSet('~=1.4.5')
print([s1.contains(v) for v in ['1.4', '1.9', '2.0']])
print([s2.contains(v) for v in ['1.4.5', '1.4.9', '1.5']])

all_cases.description: SpecifierSet excludes pre-releases by default and includes them when requested.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import SpecifierSet
s = SpecifierSet('>=1.0')
print(s.contains('1.1a1'))
print(s.contains('1.1a1', prereleases=True))
print(s.contains('1.1.dev1', prereleases=False))

all_cases.description: Invalid specifier text raises InvalidSpecifier.
Requirement parses normalized names, sorted normalized extras, and canonical specifiers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
r = Requirement('My_Pkg[PDF,security-extra] >=1.0, <2')
print(r.name)
print(sorted(r.extras))
print(str(r.specifier))
print(str(r))

all_cases.description: Requirement equality and hashing use normalized names and sorted extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Requirement
a = Requirement('Pkg_Name[B,A]>=1')
b = Requirement('pkg-name[a,b] >= 1.0.0')
print(a == b)
print(len({a, b}))
print(str(b))

all_cases.description: Invalid requirement strings raise InvalidRequirement.
Marker evaluates equality and version comparisons against a caller environment.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.11', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('os_name == "nt"').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker honors boolean precedence and parentheses.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
env = {'python_version': '3.9', 'sys_platform': 'linux', 'os_name': 'posix'}
print(Marker('python_version >= "3.8" and sys_platform == "linux"').evaluate(env))
print(Marker('python_version < "3.8" and (sys_platform == "linux" or os_name == "posix")').evaluate(env))

all_cases.description: Marker evaluates membership operators and requested extras.
all_cases.test_code:
from minipackaging import Marker
print(Marker('sys_platform in "linux darwin"').evaluate({'sys_platform': 'linux'}))
print(Marker('extra == "security-extra"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))
print(Marker('extra == "docs"').evaluate({}, requested_extras={'Security_Extra'}))

all_cases.description: Marker syntax errors and missing environment variables use the documented exceptions.
Candidate-order permutation preserves selected versions and semantic dependency edge facts when URL requirements are mixed with markers and extras.

Requirement parser fields, marker applicability, specifier membership, and requirement satisfaction drive the same selected candidate across equivalent metadata variants without counting primitive requirement-object deduplication.
Environment markers on candidate dependencies switch only the applicable dependency closure across fresh fixture variants.
all_cases.expected_output:
1 True False
1 False True
1 True False
1 False True
all_cases.actual:
1 True False
1 False True
1 True False
1 False True
all_cases.expected:
1 True False
1 False True
1 True False
1 False True

all_cases.description: Dependency edges and reverse dependents are inverse projections of the same URL, extras, and marker-qualified requirement facts.

Conflicting root and transitive constraints derive the same selected version, excluded versions, and dependents after permutation; primitive equivalent-root deduplication is not counted here unless projections diverge.
Direct URL requirement serialization and reparsing preserve parsed fields, marker applicability, extras propagation, and resolver-visible dependency closure without asserting exact whitespace.
Prerelease handling is identical between direct specifier checks and transitive resolver candidate selection.
Version-valued marker comparisons use the shared Version ordering when deciding whether root and dependency requirements participate in resolution.
A failed or tolerated invalid URL-plus-specifier metadata parse must leave inputs and parsed objects unchanged, and later valid metadata must resolve normally.

Equivalent requirement spellings must project to the same selected versions and exclusions independent of root and candidate order; primitive set-size deduplication is left to the unit equality/hash case.
Parent extras activate URL dependency requirements, and extras on dependency requirements activate child optional dependencies.
Parsed requirement objects can be reused across fresh environment fixture variants and candidate permutations without retaining prior resolution state.
